key_insight: Physics-augmented neural networks can be exported as standalone user material models and run inside explicit finite element solvers for hyperelastic impact simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical data-driven constitutive modeling in Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss without needing a custom solver, supporting more realistic large-strain material response in engineering impact analysis.
method: Train hyperelastic PANNs in PyTorch with embedded physical constraints.
Transfer the pretrained architecture and weights into autogenerated Fortran user material routines.
Reduce runtime cost by using more efficient activations such as SQuarePlus instead of SoftPlus.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an example impact simulation showing the generated PANN user material reproduces nonlinear hyperelastic behavior under large strains, and discusses evaluation-cost impacts of architecture choices.
watch_item: Deployment still depends on careful network design and efficient activation choices to keep explicit-solver evaluations computationally tractable.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
